evals.prompt: Write integration tests for the POST /routes endpoint.
evals.expected_output: Creates PostRouteTests class with [Collection(nameof(HikingLogTier0Collection))] and primary constructor inheriting IntegrationTest(factory). Two tests: PostRoute_WhenValid_Returns201 (uses RouteFaker().Generate(), asserts Status201Created) and PostRoute_WhenInvalid_Returns400 (posts empty object, asserts Status400BadRequest). No IAsyncLifetime re-implementation. CreateClient() used for the HTTP client.
assertions: Creates PostRouteTests class with [Collection(nameof(HikingLogTier0Collection))] attribute
Class inherits IntegrationTest(factory) via primary constructor
Has a test asserting Status201Created for a valid POST
Has a test asserting Status400BadRequest for an invalid POST (e.g. empty object)
Uses RouteFaker().Generate() for valid test data
Uses CreateClient() to obtain the HTTP client
Does not re-implement IAsyncLifetime or InitializeAsync
Does not use IMediator or inject handlers directly in the test class

evals.prompt: Add a Tier 3 integration test to verify that AddRoute persists the route in the database.
evals.expected_output: Creates AddRouteHandlerTests class with [Collection(nameof(HikingLogTier0Collection))] inheriting IntegrationTest(factory). Test Handle_WhenValid_PersistsRoute uses factory.Services.CreateScope(), resolves ICommandHandler<AddRoute, OneOf<AddRouteResult, ValidationFailed>> via GetRequiredService, calls Handle() directly (no HTTP client). Asserts result.IsT0, then resolves HikingLogDbContext in the same scope and verifies the route exists with db.Routes.FindAsync().
assertions: Creates AddRouteHandlerTests class with [Collection(nameof(HikingLogTier0Collection))] attribute
Class inherits IntegrationTest(factory)
Test uses factory.Services.CreateScope() to obtain a DI scope
Resolves ICommandHandler<AddRoute, OneOf<AddRouteResult, ValidationFailed>> via GetRequiredService
Calls handler.Handle() directly — no HTTP client used
Asserts result.IsT0 to verify the happy path
Resolves HikingLogDbContext from the same scope to verify database persistence
Calls db.Routes.FindAsync() or equivalent to assert the record was saved

evals.prompt: Create a RouteFaker for use in the Routes integration tests.
evals.expected_output: Creates RouteFaker : Faker<CreateRouteRequest> with base("nl") constructor for Dutch locale. RuleFor for all required properties: Name (f.Address.City() + "pad"), Code ("LAW " + random int), Country (fixed "Nederland"), TotalDistanceKm (random decimal 50–600), Description (f.Lorem.Sentence()). Class placed in tests/HikingLog.IntegrationTests/Routes/Fakers/RouteFaker.cs. XML doc <summary> on class and constructor.
assertions: Creates RouteFaker class inheriting Faker<CreateRouteRequest>
Passes "nl" locale to the base constructor
Defines RuleFor for Name property
Defines RuleFor for Code property
Defines RuleFor for Country property (fixed or randomized)
Defines RuleFor for TotalDistanceKm with a positive decimal value
Defines RuleFor for Description property
File is placed under tests/HikingLog.IntegrationTests/
XML doc <summary> on the class and constructor

evals.prompt: Write all integration tests for the GET /routes and GET /routes/{id} endpoints.
evals.expected_output: Creates two test classes. GetRoutesTests: one test GetRoutes_Returns200 asserting Status200OK on GET /routes. GetRouteTests: two tests — GetRoute_WhenExists_Returns200 (seeds a route via POST first, then GETs the Location header) and GetRoute_WhenNotFound_Returns404 (GETs /routes/99999). Both classes have [Collection(nameof(HikingLogTier0Collection))] and inherit IntegrationTest(factory).
assertions: Creates GetRoutesTests class with [Collection(nameof(HikingLogTier0Collection))] attribute
GetRoutesTests has a test asserting Status200OK for GET /routes
Creates GetRouteTests class with [Collection(nameof(HikingLogTier0Collection))] attribute
GetRouteTests has a test asserting Status200OK when the route exists
The 200 test seeds a route via POST before performing the GET (not a hardcoded id)
GetRouteTests has a test asserting Status404NotFound for a non-existent id
Both classes inherit IntegrationTest(factory)

evals.prompt: Add integration tests for the DELETE /etappes/{id} endpoint.
evals.expected_output: Creates DeleteEtappeTests class with [Collection(nameof(HikingLogTier0Collection))] inheriting IntegrationTest(factory). Two tests: DeleteEtappe_WhenExists_Returns204 (seeds a route + etappe via POST, then DELETEs, asserts Status204NoContent) and DeleteEtappe_WhenNotFound_Returns404 (DELETEs /etappes/99999, asserts Status404NotFound). Uses EtappeFaker for seeding. No IAsyncLifetime re-implementation.
assertions: Creates DeleteEtappeTests class with [Collection(nameof(HikingLogTier0Collection))] attribute
Class inherits IntegrationTest(factory)
Has a test asserting Status204NoContent for deleting an existing etappe
The 204 test seeds a parent Route via POST before creating the Etappe (FK dependency)
The 204 test seeds an Etappe via POST before deleting it
Has a test asserting Status404NotFound for deleting a non-existent etappe
Uses EtappeFaker (or equivalent) for generating test data
Does not re-implement IAsyncLifetime
